challenge_summary: Pass-6 reconciliation approves the latest boundary tightening. The generic static IaC security validator has been restored to strict baseline behavior with no import, parser, branch, or test path for comment-based exception bypasses. The security-exceptions helper is consumed only by runtime qualification-context validation, and the focused tests no longer cover or preserve a static annotation bypass. The final endpoint lifecycle remains session-only SecurityControl=Ignore: absent at rest, added before public endpoint enable and network-rule add, removed after endpoint disable, and verified as publicNetworkAccess Disabled with the tag absent. Canonical JSON, governance Markdown, plans, ADR, docs, workflow, launcher, validators, and tests remain consistent; all prior findings and the pass-5 approval remain resolved. No new fail-open path or contradiction was found. Unexecuted live Azure, OIDC, RBAC, data-plane readiness, apply, destroy, and authority-return behavior remains residual risk only.
compact_for_parent: Pass 6 (governance-reconciliation) | LOW | 0 must_fix, 0 should_fix | Strict static baseline and runtime-only exception boundary verified

prior_pass_dispositions.evidence: All pass-5 surface hashes remain unchanged except the intended security-exceptions test update and the new boundary files added to this pass. The strict static validator now has no dependency on tools/scripts/_lib/security-exceptions.mjs and no comment-annotation parser; the runtime helper remains isolated to validate-vnext-qualification-context.mjs. The approved session-only endpoint lifecycle and all pass-5 conclusions remain intact.

prior_finding_dispositions.evidence: Preview and apply run validate-vnext-qualification-context.mjs --security-exception-only after IPv4 resolution and immediately before the transient exclusion, endpoint enable, and one-/32 add. Local withFirewall validates before and after IPv4 resolution. The runtime validator requires exactly one current governance exception with the approved id, control, scope, positive lifetime capped at 24 hours, and at least 75 minutes remaining.

Canonical JSON, governance Markdown, both plans, ADR, and docs continue to state that JV - Allowed Locations is Audit and explicitly permits swedencentral, germanywestcentral, westeurope, and global. No reviewed location artifact changed since pass 5.

Base tag validation remains the exact approved ten-key contract. SecurityControl is not accepted as a static/base IaC exception; it is absent from bootstrap and workload IaC at rest and exists only as the runtime session exclusion validated by the endpoint lifecycle contract.

Boundary validation after IPv4 resolution remains present in both workflow jobs and local withFirewall. Focused lifecycle tests still include the expiry-crossing case that prevents Azure commands from running when the second validation fails.

Final lifecycle remains session-only official SecurityControl=Ignore: bootstrap and workload IaC are Disabled/default-deny with no SecurityControl tag; preview/apply/local sessions add the tag before endpoint enable and network-rule add, remove the /32, disable the endpoint, remove the tag, and verify both Disabled and tag absence.
ADR 0001 remains consistent with canonical JSON, governance Markdown, both plans, and operator documentation on the allowed-locations Audit assignment and swedencentral allowance.

evidence: tools/scripts/validate-iac-security-baseline.mjs imports only reporter, glob helpers, and regex helpers; it imports no security exception helper and has no annotation parser or suppression branch.

Every matching Bicep/Terraform baseline anti-pattern calls Reporter.error directly. The isolated negative probe with a former security-exception comment and publicNetworkAccess Enabled failed with a baseline violation.
npm run validate:iac-security-baseline scanned 2 Bicep and 6 Terraform files with 0 errors and 0 warnings.
runtime_evidence: tools/scripts/_lib/security-exceptions.mjs is imported only by tools/scripts/validate-vnext-qualification-context.mjs and tools/tests/security-exceptions.test.mjs.

validate-vnext-qualification-context.mjs --security-exception-only validates the governance exception identity, control, scope, 24-hour maximum, active window, 75-minute margin, and endpoint lifecycle arrays before endpoint sessions.
test_evidence: tools/tests/security-exceptions.test.mjs contains runtime current-record and at-rest bootstrap assertions only.
Repository search found no security-exception: annotation marker in the reviewed source, tests, IaC, artifacts, plans, or docs.

residual_risks: Live Azure Policy evaluation of the SecurityControl exclusion across storage account update and network-rule PATCH calls was not executed. The reviewed ordering is fail-closed and static validation no longer accepts comment exceptions, but live policy behavior remains deployment-phase evidence.

GitHub Environment approval, OIDC token exchange, Azure RBAC propagation, Azure CLI runtime calls, storage data-plane readiness, apply, destroy, and authority return were not executed in this review and remain residual qualification risk rather than source findings.

This pass reconciles the committed COMPLETE policy snapshot and does not re-query Azure. The snapshot retains 33 effective assignments and 18 Deny blockers; any policy, exception, or workflow refresh invalidates this review.

The approved exception expires at 2026-07-16T12:50:34Z. Runtime validation requires at least 75 minutes remaining, so a later qualification attempt must refresh governance evidence before any endpoint session.

validation_evidence.iac_security_baseline: npm run validate:iac-security-baseline: 8 files checked, 0 errors, 0 warnings
validation_evidence.static_annotation_bypass_probe: Former security-exception comment plus publicNetworkAccess Enabled was rejected with a baseline violation and nonzero exit.
